opening: Somewhere in the wide quiet desert, a very small whirlwind woke up with a very big dream.
ending:
Zephyr settled over the city — not as a storm, but as a story the wind would tell for a long time.

Thanks for playing.

beats.text: Hmm. Not much out here. Just me, the sand, and… is that a rabbit? Sorry, rabbit.
Oh! I can take that with me. Nice.
You know what? I bet the city has more stuff. Better stuff. Tall stuff.
They say you gotta start small. Good news: I am extremely small.
I think… I'm big enough to leave. First stop: those mountains. Then? The city. Watch out, world.
Up. Why is it always up? Couldn't my dream be downhill?
Solid. Heavy. Like my determination. Mostly sand, though.
A whole truck! I'm basically a traffic hazard now. Love that for me.
The desert feels far away already. Kind of miss the quiet. Kind of don't.
So many leaves! So many things that rustle! This is EXCELLENT.
Okay. Note to self: not everything wants to join the whirlwind.
If I spin a little harder… oh HO. I'm FAST.
I can smell pavement. Or maybe that's just more dust. Either way: almost there.
Houses. Cars. People who definitely have homeowner's insurance. Deep breath. Spin.
Four wheels, one whirlwind. Fair trade.
I just ate a house. A small one! Still! Mom would be proud. If I had a mom. Or a house.
The skyline's right there. Tall. Sparkly. Mine, probably.
Okay. City. Big buildings. Don't panic. Panic spinning is still spinning.
Public transit has entered the vortex.
Floor 1… floor 2… I'm basically a moving elevator.
That one. The really tall one. That's the dream, isn't it? Always was.
I made it. I'm big. I'm here. …Huh. The city looks kind of cozy from inside a skyscraper. Hello, world. I grew up.
